claims.claim: Israel's standard VAT rate is 18% in 2026 (raised from 17% on 1 January 2025; a proposed bump to 19% was rejected and it stayed at 18%).
claims.raw_snippet: שיעור המע"מ הסטנדרטי הוא 18% (עלה מ-17% ב-1 בינואר 2025); הצעה להעלאה ל-19% נדחתה.

claims.claim: VAT reporting is bi-monthly for businesses with annual turnover up to ₪1,775,000 (the threshold updated 1 January 2026, indexed annually and published by the Tax Authority) and monthly above it. Reports/payments are due within 15 days of the end of the reporting period.
claims.raw_snippet: עסקים שמחזור עסקאותיהם בשנה הקובעת הוא עד 1,775,000 ש"ח (נכון ל-01.01.2026) יגישו דוח אחת לחודשיים; מעל הסף - דיווח חודשי. עד 15 ימים אחרי תום תקופת הדיווח.

claims.claim: The חשבוניות ישראל allocation-number (מספר הקצאה) regime requires an allocation number for tax-deductible business invoices above ₪10,000 (ex-VAT) from 1 January 2026, dropping to ₪5,000 from 1 June 2026, or the buyer cannot reclaim the VAT input. The threshold stepped down from ₪25,000 (2024) and ₪20,000 (2025).
claims.raw_snippet: חוק חשבוניות ישראל: מ-1.1.2026 נדרש מספר הקצאה לחשבונית מעל 10,000 ש"ח (לפני מע"מ), ומ-1.6.2026 הסף יורד ל-5,000 ש"ח.

claims.claim: Bituach Leumi advance payments and income-tax advances (mikdamot) for the self-employed are due the 15th of each month. Employer annual 102 reports are filed in February.
claims.raw_snippet: מקדמות ביטוח לאומי ומקדמות מס הכנסה לעצמאים משולמות עד ה-15 בכל חודש.

claims.claim: Israeli 5786/2026 holiday dates (one yom tov per holiday in Israel): Purim 3 March 2026 (Jerusalem 4 March); Pesach eve 1 April, first day (15 Nisan) 2 April, Chol HaMoed 3-7 April (7 April is the eve of the last day, a short day), last day (Shvi'i shel Pesach, 21 Nisan) 8 April, with 9 April being Isru Chag (a normal workday in Israel; the 22 Nisan diaspora-only eighth day); Yom HaShoah 14 April; Yom HaZikaron 21 April; Yom HaAtzmaut 22 April; Lag BaOmer 5 May; Yom Yerushalayim 15 May (28 Iyar); Shavuot 22 May; Tu BiShvat 2 February. Iron Swords Memorial Day is fixed at 24 Tishrei (e.g., 5 October 2026), moved to Sunday 25 Tishrei if it falls on Shabbat.
claims.raw_snippet: Israel holidays 2026 (i=on): Purim Mar 3; Pesach I Apr 2, Pesach VII (last yom tov in Israel) Apr 8, Apr 9 = Isru Chag (workday); Yom HaShoah Apr 14; Yom HaZikaron Apr 21; Yom HaAtzmaut Apr 22; Lag BaOmer May 5; Yom Yerushalayim May 15; Shavuot May 22.

claims.claim: Iron Swords Memorial Day (יום הזיכרון לחללי מלחמת חרבות ברזל) was established by Knesset/government decision in 2024 and is fixed at 24 Tishrei.
claims.raw_snippet: יום הזיכרון לחללי מלחמת חרבות ברזל נקבע בכ"ד בתשרי בהחלטת ממשלה משנת 2024.

claims.claim: Resource/reference URLs cited in the skill and reference files for calendar and statistics lookups.
claims.raw_snippet: Reference URLs: https://www.boi.org.il https://www.cbs.gov.il https://www.hebcal.com https://www.hebcal.com/?i=on https://www.hebcal.com/converter https://www.hebcal.com/hebcal https://www.hebcal.com/home/195/jewish-calendar-rest-api

claims.claim: Israeli formatting conventions and illustrative figures: currency written symbol-first (e.g. ₪1,234.56); Chol HaMoed and August slowdowns of roughly 30-50% and businesses at 50-70% capacity are illustrative estimates, not measured statistics; ID/business numbers are 9 digits; postcodes 7 digits.
claims.raw_snippet: Illustrative: currency format ₪1,234.56 (symbol before amount); response-rate slowdowns of 30-50% during Chol HaMoed and businesses at 50-70% capacity in August are rules-of-thumb, not statistics.
